summary.intent: Add Firecrawl web scraping/search integration to the moltis project, modeled after how openclaw implements it, with a feature gate to allow disabling it.
summary.outcome: Full Firecrawl integration was implemented and merged: a standalone scrape tool, a search provider variant, a web_fetch fallback, config schema/validation/template updates, feature flags (default-on), and gateway wiring. All 640 tests pass, clippy clean, PR created.

repo: openclaw's firecrawl plugin lives at /Users/penso/code/openclaw/extensions/firecrawl/ and served as the reference implementation
moltis feature flags follow a default-on pattern in crates/cli/Cargo.toml and propagate down through gateway → tools crates
Config additions require changes in 3 files: schema.rs (struct/enum), validate.rs (schema map + semantic warnings), and template.rs (commented-out example)
macOS builds exclude CUDA features; llama-cpp crate has unrelated build issues on macOS that affect cargo clippy on the full workspace
Clippy lint `clippy::collapsible-if` was triggered by nested if-let blocks in web_search.rs and web_fetch.rs

code.finding: Extraction chain: Readability (local) → Firecrawl fallback (if configured and readability yields <10% of HTML body) → basic HTML cleanup
New module: FirecrawlScrapeTool + shared helpers firecrawl_scrape(), firecrawl_search(), resolve_api_key(). 21 tests using mockito.
Semantic warning pattern: warn when search provider = firecrawl but no API key is configured
SearchProvider enum extended with Firecrawl variant; with_firecrawl_config() builder pattern matches existing with_brave_config() style

workflow: The initial agent search ran against the wrong path and had to be manually corrected with a direct Grep
Incremental build verification after each major step (compile check, then tests, then clippy, then fmt) caught the missing Debug derive on ScrapeResult early
Running clippy per-crate (--package) avoids unrelated workspace failures from CUDA/llama-cpp crates on macOS
cargo fmt uses pinned nightly toolchain in this repo

friction: Initial agent search searched the wrong repository path, requiring manual correction
Missing `#[derive(Debug)]` on ScrapeResult caused test compilation failure, caught only when running tests
Clippy on the full workspace fails on macOS due to unrelated llama-cpp/CUDA crate issues, requiring per-crate clippy invocation
Two collapsible-if clippy warnings had to be fixed in web_search.rs and web_fetch.rs after initial implementation
open_items: Firecrawl feature is default-on; there may be value in revisiting whether it should be opt-in given it adds an external API dependency
The <10% threshold for triggering the Firecrawl fallback in web_fetch is a heuristic that may need tuning based on real-world usage
No integration/E2E tests for the actual Firecrawl API calls (only mockito unit tests); real API smoke tests could be added
The firecrawl_search() function in web_search.rs was added but no search provider UI/docs were updated
